title: How to Survive the AI Shock
gist: Davidson and Slaughter, in the accessible pre-paywall text, frame AI's economic promise through the lens of America's multi-decade labor-productivity slowdown, arguing weak productivity growth has driven poor income growth and fueled political polarization; the piece is billed as 'A Policy Playbook to Avert Political Crisis' but its specific AI policy proposals are cut off by the paywall.

insights.claim: The United States, like many other countries, has endured decades of slow growth in labor productivity — the amount of goods and services produced per worker — which the authors call the single most important measure of a country's overall economic success.
insights.speaker: James A. Davidson
insights.so_what: Sets productivity growth as the master variable the authors use to judge AI's economic payoff — a lens for evaluating whether AI-driven productivity claims are being tested against the right benchmark.
insights.quote: Productivity, the amount of goods and services produced per worker, is the single most important measure of a country’s overall economic success.
insights.locator: Productivity, the amount of goods and services produced per worker

insights.claim: Slow productivity growth has caused poor growth in average incomes, which in turn has fueled political turmoil globally and contributed to escalating political polarization in the United States, per Davidson and Slaughter.
insights.speaker: James A. Davidson
insights.so_what: Establishes the causal chain (weak productivity to weak income growth to polarization) the authors use to argue AI's economic and political stakes are linked — relevant for assessing political-risk exposure to a stalled or mismanaged AI productivity transition.
insights.quote: Slow productivity growth has meant poor growth in average incomes, which in turn has fueled much of the political turmoil manifested across the globe in the last generation.
insights.locator: Slow productivity growth has meant poor growth in average incomes

insights.claim: Davidson and Slaughter open by stating that 'the dawning age of artificial intelligence holds great promise for the world economy and for the United States,' positioning AI as a potential remedy for the productivity slowdown they describe, ahead of the policy recommendations promised in the piece's subtitle ('A Policy Playbook to Avert Political Crisis').
insights.speaker: James A. Davidson
insights.so_what: Flags this as an AI-policy-prescription piece; the specific playbook is paywalled but the framing signals the authors view AI-driven productivity gains as a variable to manage politically, not just economically.
insights.quote: The dawning age of artificial intelligence holds great promise for the world economy and for the United States.
insights.locator: The dawning age of artificial intelligence
